ba_name: Corbin Cash Single Barrel Merced Rye 7 Year
reasoning: 100% Merced Rye (California heirloom), 7 yrs single barrel, 65% ABV cask strength. Tasting notes: intense rye spice, peppermint, herbal, dark cherry, baking spices. 100% rye + max age + max proof = peak rye intensity.

75/20/5 organic rye/Purple Karma Barley/Buckwheat. 100 proof. No age or tasting notes available — scored from mash construction alone. 75% rye is on the higher side, no corn so dry. Re-score after tasting.

ba_name: Hautes Glaces Vulson Rye Eau de Vie
reasoning: French Alpine RYE EAU-DE-VIE (not whiskey!) from native mountain rye, cellared but essentially unaged. Tasting: petrichor, hazelnut, licorice nose; rye bread, roasted-agave, gentian palate — 'blind-tastes mezcal-like.' Unaged means near-zero oak/vanilla; the herbal/savory eau-de-vie character means it's a poor substitute for classic rye whiskey in cocktails. Score is honest to the spirit; recipes calling for rye whiskey should not pick this.

ba_name: Heaven Hill Grain to Glass Kentucky Straight Rye Whiskey
reasoning: 63/24/13 rye/corn/malt — spicier end of Kentucky rye. ~6-7 yrs, 61.6% ABV cask strength. No specific tasting notes available, but the mashbill + age + proof profile points to a classic high-rye Kentucky whiskey.

95/5 rye/malted barley, 5 yrs, DOUBLE-oaked (charred new oak then re-barreled in toasted/charred). Tasting: toasted marshmallow, vanilla, butterscotch, minty rye spice. Double-oak treatment pushes oak and vanilla to max.

ba_name: Savage & Cooke Cask Finished Rye Whiskey
reasoning: 51/45/4 rye/corn/barley (mid-rye), 3+ yrs #3 char oak, Grenache wine barrel finish. 50% ABV. Grenache finish adds wine fruit; high corn moderates the rye intensity.

90/10 rye/malted barley, char #3 new oak, multi-year aged. Award-winning Missouri straight rye (ACSA Best in Class 2018). 45% ABV. Mashbill drives high spice; no corn = dry; no specific tasting notes available.

ba_name: Still Austin Cask Strength Straight Rye Whiskey
reasoning: 100% Texas rye, 58% ABV cask strength, 3+ yrs. Tasting: cherry cobbler, buttery cinnamon nose. Texas climate extracts vanilla aggressively; 100% rye + cask strength = max spice and body; cherry note = high fruit.
